deterministic_setup.reason:
The artifact POM marks servlet-api as provided, so Gradle will not bring javax.servlet.* onto the test classpath transitively; existing Jackrabbit WebDAV coverage exercises servlet-facing classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, WebdavRequestImpl, WebdavResponseImpl, InputContextImpl, and OutputContex
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported native-image failures are assertion failures in existing AbstractLocatorFactory tests, so local Gradle/Native Image verification must still confirm whether behavior changed between 1.5.0 and 2.0-beta4 rather than treating setup as the root cause.
No Docker service appears necessary; the library can be covered with in-process WebDAV/XML/request-response tests rather than a live WebDAV server.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Jackrabbit WebDAV exposes servlet-based server/request/response APIs and declares javax.servlet:servlet-api as a provided dependency, so tests that meaningfully cover normal WebDAV server usage need the servlet API added explicitly.
